turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bgGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#071021" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#132238" />
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sunGlow" cx="50%" cy="40%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff9d6" stop-opacity="1"/>
      <stop offset="40%" stop-color="#ffe08a" stop-opacity="0.9"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff9f3b" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="lampGlow" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fffbe6" stop-opacity="1"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#ffd17a" stop-opacity="0.6"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff7b2b" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="8" result="b" />
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="b" />
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic" />
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#bgGrad)" />

  <!-- Distant subtle stars -->
  <g fill="#fff7d9" fill-opacity="0.12">
    <circle cx="70" cy="60" r="2.2" />
    <circle cx="120" cy="40" r="1.6" />
    <circle cx="430" cy="80" r="1.8" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="50" r="1.4" />
    <circle cx="250" cy="30" r="2.0" />
    <circle cx="460" cy="180" r="1.6" />
  </g>

  <!-- Central glow representing illumination -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="170" r="110" fill="url(#sunGlow)" filter="url(#soft)" />

  <!-- Radiant rays -->
  <g transform="translate(256,170)" fill="#fff3b8" opacity="0.92">
    <polygon points="0,-78 8,-132 -8,-132" transform="rotate(0)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 12,-128 -12,-128" transform="rotate(22.5)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 10,-132 -10,-132" transform="rotate(45)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 12,-128 -12,-128" transform="rotate(67.5)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 8,-132 -8,-132" transform="rotate(90)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 10,-132 -10,-132" transform="rotate(112.5)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 12,-128 -12,-128" transform="rotate(135)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 8,-132 -8,-132" transform="rotate(157.5)" />
  </g>

  <!-- Stylized open book below the glow -->
  <g transform="translate(256,350)">
    <!-- left page -->
    <path d="M-170,0 L-10,-70 L-10,0 Z" fill="#f8f0e6" stroke="#d9c9b6" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- right page -->
    <path d="M170,0 L10,-70 L10,0 Z" fill="#f8f0e6" stroke="#d9c9b6" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- page crease / spine -->
    <path d="M-10,-70 L10,-70 L10,0 L-10,0 Z" fill="#ecdcc6" stroke="#d0bfa6" stroke-width="1" />

    <!-- subtle page lines (no letters) -->
    <g stroke="#e6d9c9" stroke-width="1.4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9">
      <path d="M-150,-10 L-20,-10" />
      <path d="M-150,-2 L-20,-2" />
      <path d="M-150,6 L-20,6" />

      <path d="M150,-10 L20,-10" />
      <path d="M150,-2 L20,-2" />
      <path d="M150,6 L20,6" />
    </g>

    <!-- shadow under book -->
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="18" rx="160" ry="24" fill="#000" opacity="0.12" />
  </g>

  <!-- Small floating sparks between book and glow -->
  <g fill="#fff8c7" opacity="0.95">
    <circle cx="230" cy="230" r="3.2" />
    <circle cx="280" cy="210" r="2.6" />
    <circle cx="260" cy="250" r="2.8" />
    <circle cx="300" cy="190" r="2.2" />
    <circle cx="220" cy="200" r="2.0" />
  </g>

  <!-- Decorative soft lamp silhouette at center of glow to emphasize light-source idea -->
  <g transform="translate(256,170)">
    <path d="M0,-38 C18,-38 34,-24 34,-6 C34,18 -28,36 -34,36 C-40,36 -34,18 -34,-6 C-34,-24 -18,-38 0,-38 Z" fill="#ffd78a" opacity="0.95" filter="url(#soft)"/>
    <path d="M0,-22 C10,-22 18,-16 18,-8 C18,6 -14,18 -18,18 C-22,18 -18,6 -18,-8 C-18,-16 -10,-22 0,-22 Z" fill="#fff6cd" opacity="0.95"/>
  </g>

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bgGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#071021" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#132238" />
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sunGlow" cx="50%" cy="40%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff9d6" stop-opacity="1"/>
      <stop offset="40%" stop-color="#ffe08a" stop-opacity="0.9"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff9f3b" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="lampGlow" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fffbe6" stop-opacity="1"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#ffd17a" stop-opacity="0.6"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff7b2b" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="8" result="b" />
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="b" />
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic" />
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#bgGrad)" />

  <!-- Distant subtle stars -->
  <g fill="#fff7d9" fill-opacity="0.12">
    <circle cx="70" cy="60" r="2.2" />
    <circle cx="120" cy="40" r="1.6" />
    <circle cx="430" cy="80" r="1.8" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="50" r="1.4" />
    <circle cx="250" cy="30" r="2.0" />
    <circle cx="460" cy="180" r="1.6" />
  </g>

  <!-- Central glow representing illumination -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="170" r="110" fill="url(#sunGlow)" filter="url(#soft)" />

  <!-- Radiant rays -->
  <g transform="translate(256,170)" fill="#fff3b8" opacity="0.92">
    <polygon points="0,-78 8,-132 -8,-132" transform="rotate(0)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 12,-128 -12,-128" transform="rotate(22.5)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 10,-132 -10,-132" transform="rotate(45)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 12,-128 -12,-128" transform="rotate(67.5)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 8,-132 -8,-132" transform="rotate(90)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 10,-132 -10,-132" transform="rotate(112.5)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 12,-128 -12,-128" transform="rotate(135)" />
    <polygon points="0,-78 8,-132 -8,-132" transform="rotate(157.5)" />
  </g>

  <!-- Stylized open book below the glow -->
  <g transform="translate(256,350)">
    <!-- left page -->
    <path d="M-170,0 L-10,-70 L-10,0 Z" fill="#f8f0e6" stroke="#d9c9b6" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- right page -->
    <path d="M170,0 L10,-70 L10,0 Z" fill="#f8f0e6" stroke="#d9c9b6" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- page crease / spine -->
    <path d="M-10,-70 L10,-70 L10,0 L-10,0 Z" fill="#ecdcc6" stroke="#d0bfa6" stroke-width="1" />

    <!-- subtle page lines (no letters) -->
    <g stroke="#e6d9c9" stroke-width="1.4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9">
      <path d="M-150,-10 L-20,-10" />
      <path d="M-150,-2 L-20,-2" />
      <path d="M-150,6 L-20,6" />

      <path d="M150,-10 L20,-10" />
      <path d="M150,-2 L20,-2" />
      <path d="M150,6 L20,6" />
    </g>

    <!-- shadow under book -->
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="18" rx="160" ry="24" fill="#000" opacity="0.12" />
  </g>

  <!-- Small floating sparks between book and glow -->
  <g fill="#fff8c7" opacity="0.95">
    <circle cx="230" cy="230" r="3.2" />
    <circle cx="280" cy="210" r="2.6" />
    <circle cx="260" cy="250" r="2.8" />
    <circle cx="300" cy="190" r="2.2" />
    <circle cx="220" cy="200" r="2.0" />
  </g>

  <!-- Decorative soft lamp silhouette at center of glow to emphasize light-source idea -->
  <g transform="translate(256,170)">
    <path d="M0,-38 C18,-38 34,-24 34,-6 C34,18 -28,36 -34,36 C-40,36 -34,18 -34,-6 C-34,-24 -18,-38 0,-38 Z" fill="#ffd78a" opacity="0.95" filter="url(#soft)"/>
    <path d="M0,-22 C10,-22 18,-16 18,-8 C18,6 -14,18 -18,18 C-22,18 -18,6 -18,-8 C-18,-16 -10,-22 0,-22 Z" fill="#fff6cd" opacity="0.95"/>
  </g>

</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Designing an SVG for Illuminism**

I'm working on an image that represents "illuminism," which relates to enlightenment or illumination. I need to create this drawing using only SVG, avoiding any text or numbers. Several ideas come to mind, like a radiant sun, a torch, or a lamp with beams of light. I want to depict light shining on a dark background, possibly revealing a stylized brain or an open book without using letters. I'll keep it simple and focus on a central glowing lamp or candle.**Formatting JSON for SVG**

I need to make sure I properly escape quotes in the JSON object for the SVG. The JSON should have double quotes around its values, and any double quotes within the SVG string must be escaped. I’m considering using single quotes inside the SVG since attributes usually need double quotes, so that's tricky. My plan is to design a 512x512 SVG with a dark bluish background and a stylized warm yellow/orange lamp. I want to include small stars and an open book silhouette below the lamp, ensuring there’s no actual text, just page lines.**Creating a Clear SVG Design**

I’m simplifying my SVG design to feature a central stylized torch or lit candle on a pedestal, surrounded by a soft radial gradient glow and rays. I could outline an abstract human head silhouette with minimal details. Maybe I’ll include an open book with a radiant sun above it, representing enlightenment without any text. I’ll use geometric shapes like rectangles for the book and a glowing sun. The design will involve specific SVG elements for gradients and shadow effects while ensuring everything is properly formatted for JSON.**Constructing SVG Content for JSON**

I’m starting to build the SVG content. I’ll create a radial gradient for the glow and a background rectangle with a dark color, possibly a gradient fill. The sun will be represented as a circle with an inner blur for a halo, accompanied by rays. For the open book, I’ll draw two polygons at the bottom center to represent pages, colored beige with shading, and add small stars around the design. Finally, I need to format everything properly in JSON with escaped quotes, ensuring clarity and simplicity while avoiding any text.**Assembling SVG Content**

I'm ready to assemble the SVG content! I need to make sure I close all tags correctly and include a soft glow filter in the design. The filter will use a Gaussian blur and merge it with the graphic. I’ll implement a gradient background and position the sun at the center with its rays around it. The book will group nicely at the bottom center. Once I have everything ready, I’ll properly escape the quotes and prepare the final JSON output.

I want to include small stars and an open book silhouette below the lamp, ensuring there’s no actual text, just page lines.**Creating a Clear SVG Design**

I’m simplifying my SVG design to feature a central stylized torch or lit candle on a pedestal, surrounded by a soft radial gradient glow and rays. I could outline an abstract human head silhouette with minimal details. Maybe I’ll include an open book with a radiant sun above it, representing enlightenment without any text. I’ll use geometric shapes like rectangles for the book and a glowing sun. The design will involve specific SVG elements for gradients and shadow effects while ensuring everything is properly formatted for JSON.**Constructing SVG Content for JSON**

I’m starting to build the SVG content. I’ll create a radial gradient for the glow and a background rectangle with a dark color, possibly a gradient fill. The sun will be represented as a circle with an inner blur for a halo, accompanied by rays. For the open book, I’ll draw two polygons at the bottom center to represent pages, colored beige with shading, and add small stars around the design. Finally, I need to format everything properly in JSON with escaped quotes, ensuring clarity and simplicity while avoiding any text.**Assembling SVG Content**

I'm ready to assemble the SVG content! I need to make sure I close all tags correctly and include a soft glow filter in the design. The filter will use a Gaussian blur and merge it with the graphic. I’ll implement a gradient background and position the sun at the center with its rays around it. The book will group nicely at the bottom center. Once I have everything ready, I’ll properly escape the quotes and prepare the final JSON output.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
